notes: Eval 3 (customization spec) ties 7/7 for both configurations — current assertions don't fully capture skill value. Qualitative differences are significant (15 vs 11 test cases, batched search.lookupFields, copy-mode pageInit handling) but not measured.

The two failing assertions (Solution ID assignment in eval-0, Completion Checklist in eval-1) are structurally related: both are template-structure behaviors that only appear when the skill's templates are followed.

With-skill runs used ~40% more tokens on average due to reading SKILL.md and all referenced templates before producing output — expected overhead that pays back in structural consistency and traceability.

Baseline runs produced surprisingly high-quality content, suggesting assertions should be tightened in iteration 2. Consider adding assertions that check for: Open Questions Tracker format, Sign-Off table, template section ordering, or RTM-ready Solution IDs.
